reasoning: Croatia is a highly stable country where violent riots are extremely rare. The ACLED data indicates an average of only ~7 riots across the entire past year (a 30-day average of ~0.58). A positive resolution would require at least 16 separate 'Riot' events recorded within a single 30-day period. This would constitute an unprecedented explosion of mass violence in multiple locations and days. Recent news up to May 2025 confirms domestic stability, with elections and protests (such as boycotts) passing largely peacefully. While the uncertainty inherently increases for dates further out into late 2025, the structural stability of Croatia and the lack of any significant geopolitical or domestic catalysts make it highly improbable that the monthly riot rate will spike to 10 times its baseline formula. Probabilities are set to the minimum allowed bound (0.05) for the near-term, and slightly higher for distant dates strictly to reflect increasing epistemic uncertainty over time.
